questions.question: What is concussion?
options: A cut or graze to the scalp that only needs a plaster
A type of everyday headache that everyone gets from time to time
A permanent illness that can never improve
A mild traumatic brain injury caused by a blow or jolt to the head or body

questions.question: In a care setting, what is the most common cause of a head injury?
options: Talking for too long
Reading in poor light
Eating too quickly
A fall

questions.question: Why can you not judge how serious a head injury is just by looking at the outside of the head?
options: Because you must shave the hair first to see anything
Because concussion is a hidden injury that may leave no cut, bruise or swelling yet still affect the brain
Because a bruise always means the brain is fine
Because head injuries always look far worse than they are

questions.question: Which people are especially vulnerable to serious bleeding after even a minor bump to the head?
options: People who take anticoagulant (blood-thinning) medication
People who sleep during the day
People who wear glasses
People who eat a lot of salt

questions.question: Which best describes a care worker's role in relation to concussion?
options: To recognise, give basic first aid, monitor, escalate, and record and report
To diagnose the concussion and decide on treatment
To prescribe medicines for the head injury
To carry out brain scans before help arrives

questions.question: For at least how long should someone be watched closely after they have bumped or jolted their head?
options: At least 24 hours
Only until they stand up again
There is no need to watch them at all
Just five minutes

questions.question: What number should you call if a person shows an emergency red flag after a head injury?
options: The local pharmacy

questions.question: Which of these can be an immediate sign of concussion soon after a head injury?
options: Feeling more energetic than usual
Seeming dazed or confused and complaining of a headache
Nothing at all, because concussion never causes symptoms
A sudden and lasting improvement in eyesight

questions.question: When can the symptoms of concussion appear?
options: Never, because concussion has no symptoms
Straight away, or hours or even days later
Only ever within the first minute
Only after several months

questions.question: Why does speed matter when a head injury is serious?
options: Because head injuries are never serious and always settle on their own
Because bleeding or swelling inside the skull can be life-threatening and early hospital treatment can save a life
Because ambulances are cheaper earlier in the day
Because the person will be embarrassed if you wait

questions.question: What does anticoagulant (blood-thinning) medication do?
options: It cures concussion
It thickens the blood so bleeding stops faster
It makes the blood slower to clot, so bleeding is harder to stop
It has no effect on bleeding at all

questions.question: Which of the following is a red flag that means you should call 999 after a head injury?
options: The person has a seizure, is repeatedly vomiting, or is difficult to wake
The person has a small graze on their hand from the fall
The person asks what time lunch will be served
The person feels a little tired and wants to sit down

questions.question: As part of basic first aid, what should you encourage a person who has hurt their head to do?
options: Drive themselves to hospital
Get up and walk about to shake it off
Stay still and rest, especially if they are dizzy or unsteady
Carry on with heavy physical work

questions.question: If there is swelling or a bump after a head injury, what can you gently apply to help ease it?
options: A hot water bottle
Nothing, because swelling must never be touched
Something cold, such as an ice pack wrapped in a cloth
A tight bandage around the neck

questions.question: What should you do if someone loses consciousness after a head injury, even only briefly?
options: Ask them to walk it off
Treat it as an emergency and call 999
Wait a day to see if it happens again
Give them a cup of tea and carry on

questions.question: Why is it important to protect someone from another head injury while they are recovering from concussion?
options: Because a second head injury before the first has healed can have far more serious effects
Because a second injury would speed up their recovery
Because they must return to all normal activities at once
Because concussion is not a real injury and only feels bad

questions.question: What does the brain most need in the early days after a concussion?
options: Intense exercise
Bright lights and loud noise
Rest
A long car journey

questions.question: What should you do after a person in your care has a head injury?
options: Wait a week and then write a rough note if you still remember
Record the injury and your observations accurately, report it promptly, and follow the care plan and procedures
Say nothing and hope they recover without anyone knowing
Only tell a colleague verbally and keep no written record

questions.question: Why can a blow or jolt cause concussion inside the skull?
options: Because the skull is filled with air
Because the brain is soft and floats in fluid, so a sudden impact can make it knock against the inside of the skull
Because concussion only affects the hair and scalp
Because the brain is firmly fixed and cannot move at all

questions.question: Does a person have to be knocked out for it to be a concussion?
options: Yes, otherwise it is only a headache
Only if they are over 65
Yes, there is always a loss of consciousness
No, a person can have concussion without ever being knocked out

questions.question: Which of these is a delayed sign that may develop in the hours or days after a head injury?
options: Growing drowsiness and changes in behaviour such as becoming irritable or withdrawn
A total absence of any symptoms forever
Feeling calmer and more focused than ever
A permanent improvement in memory

questions.question: What useful information should you have ready for the paramedics?
options: The person's favourite television programme
What happened, when it happened, and the person's medicines, especially any blood thinners
How long you have worked in care
The colour of the room where it happened

questions.question: How many red flags do you need to see before you act and call 999?
options: You should wait until the next shift
At least three
A single red flag is enough to act on
All of them at once

questions.question: During basic first aid, what should you NOT do unless it is safe and part of the person's plan?
options: Encourage the person to stay still
Stay with the person
Reassure the person and keep them calm
Give the person food, drink or medicines

questions.question: What does this Concussion Awareness training provide?
options: A qualification to diagnose brain injuries
Awareness and knowledge to help you recognise and respond, without replacing supervised or first-aid training
Permission to prescribe medicines
A replacement for supervised or first-aid training

questions.question: Concussion is a mild traumatic brain injury.
options: True
False

questions.question: You can always see a concussion from the outside as a cut, bruise or swelling.
options: True
False

questions.question: A person must be knocked out for it to count as a concussion.
options: True
False

questions.question: In a care setting, most head injuries follow a fall.
options: True
False

questions.question: Concussion symptoms always appear straight away, so if someone seems fine right after a fall you do not need to keep watching them.
options: True
False

questions.question: A single red flag after a head injury is enough for you to call 999.
options: True
False

questions.question: People who take blood-thinning medication can suffer serious bleeding from even a minor bump to the head.
options: True
False

questions.question: A second head injury during recovery from concussion helps the person heal more quickly.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following are red flags that mean you should call 999 after a head injury? (Select all that apply)
options: Repeated vomiting since the injury
A seizure or fit
Feeling a little tired and wanting to sit down
Loss of consciousness, even briefly
A small graze on the hand

questions.question: Which of the following can be signs or symptoms of concussion? (Select all that apply)
options: Headache
Dizziness
Blurred vision and problems with memory
A lasting improvement in eyesight
Feeling more energetic than usual

questions.question: Which of the following are part of a care worker's role after a head injury? (Select all that apply)
options: Giving basic first aid
Prescribing treatment
Monitoring the person and escalating when needed
Diagnosing the exact brain injury
Recognising when someone may have hurt their head

questions.question: Which of the following are appropriate basic first-aid actions after a head injury? (Select all that apply)
options: Reassure the person and keep them calm
Give the person strong painkillers regardless of their plan
Gently apply something cold, such as an ice pack wrapped in a cloth, to swelling
Encourage the person to stay still and rest
Make the person walk around to prove they are fine

questions.question: Which of the following are delayed changes to watch for in the hours and days after a head injury? (Select all that apply)
options: A permanent improvement in concentration
Growing drowsiness
Feeling stronger and more alert than before the injury
A headache that comes on or gets worse
Increasing confusion and changes in behaviour

questions.question: Which of the following should be included when you record and report a head injury? (Select all that apply)
options: Your personal guess about who is to blame
The action you took
What happened, when, and how the injury occurred
A note written a week later from rough memory
The symptoms you observed and how the person is over time

questions.question: Which of the following are true about people who take anticoagulant (blood-thinning) medication? (Select all that apply)
options: You should tell the ambulance service or health professional that they take this medication
They are completely safe from bleeding after a head injury
Their blood is slower to clot, so bleeding is harder to stop
Even a small knock to the head can cause dangerous bleeding
You should wait longer than usual before seeking any medical advice

questions.question: Concussion is a mild traumatic ___ injury.
In a care setting, most head injuries follow a ___.
If you see a red flag after a head injury, you must call ___ for urgent help.
Anticoagulant medicines, also called blood ___, slow down the way the blood clots.
After a head injury, a person should be watched closely for at least ___ hours.
